summary: Built comprehensive positioning document reframing Careerspan around the killer stat: 46% of hires fail within 18 months, 90% due to behavioral mismatch—not technical competence. This means the entire $3.5B assessment industry focuses on the 10% while ignoring the 90%. Careerspan captures behavioral signal through story-based assessment where candidates reveal (to a coach helping them) rather than perform (for an evaluator judging them). Mapped to specific acquirers with tailored pitches emphasizing 'we capture the 90% aptitude tests miss.'
key_insight: The 46%/90% stat transforms the entire positioning. It's not 'Careerspan offers a better assessment.' It's 'The entire assessment industry is solving the wrong problem—10% of failures—while we solve the 90%.' This reframes Careerspan from incremental improvement to category correction.
changes_from_v1: Added 46%/90% stat as the central economic argument
Reframed around '$200B problem' (cost of bad hires at scale)
Added comparison table showing why each existing tool category misses 90%
Added Marvin Ventures partnership as credibility signal
Added 'experience paradox' (feels human, produces structured data) as key differentiator
Strengthened scalability comparison with cost-per-candidate table
Developed tailored pitches for ATS vs assessment acquirers
Updated tech references (removed GPT-4, now 'multi-agent AI architecture')
Added 'The One-Liner' crystallization: 'We capture the 90% that aptitude tests miss'
